message: Node started: Iterate through prime numbers p starting from 2. For each p, determine if there exists an integer n such that n^4 + 1 is congruent to 0 modulo p. A prime p allows a solution to n^4 + 1 = 0 mod p if and only if p = 2 or p is congruent to 1 modulo 8. For each candidate p, verify the condition by checking if there is an n in the range [0, p-1] such that (n^4 + 1) mod p = 0. Stop at the first prime p that satisfies this and output this prime p.
